data.title: Novo Nordisk slips after data for next-gen obesity drug
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) shares fell nearly 7% premarket on Monday, the most since December, after the drugmaker said its investigational next-generation obesity drug CagriSema caused 15.7% weight loss in a clinical trial of overweight or obese patients with type 2 diabetes.

The once-weekly subcutaneous CagriSema is being investigated by Novo Nordisk as a treatment for adults with overweight or obesity (REDEFINE program) and as a treatment for adults with type 2 diabetes (REIMAGINE program).

The phase 3 trial REDEFINE 2 trial evaluated once-weekly subcutaneous CagriSema over a period of 68 weeks and was based on a flexible protocol, allowing patients to modify their dosing throughout the trial.

While the trial achieved its primary endpoint, fewer than two-thirds of the participants were on the highest dose of the drug after 68 weeks.

If all people adhered to treatment with CagriSema, patients overall achieved weight loss of 15.7% after 68 weeks, compared to 31.1% with placebo. The trial included 1,206 randomized people with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes and a mean baseline body weight of 102 kg.

When applying the treatment policy estimand, CagriSema caused a weight loss of 13.7% compared to 3.4% with placebo.

The data follows disappointing results from another late-stage trial of CagriSema showing lower-than-expected weight loss. Results published in December demonstrated that the once-weekly injectable caused only up to ~20% weight loss over 68 weeks in the intent-to-treat population, failing to meet the 25% bar the Danish drugmaker set in the lead-up to the readout.

Other drugmakers developing weight loss therapies, including Eli Lilly (LLY) -0.53%, Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) +0.45%, Amgen (AMGN), Biohaven (BHVN) -1.17%, and Structure Therapeutics (GPCR) -0.13%, moved marginally in the premarket.

More on Novo Nordisk

data.title: Plus Therapeutics regains compliance with Nasdaq minimum stockholders’ equity requirement
data.description:
• Plus Therapeutics (NASDAQ:PSTV) Monday said  that it received confirmation from Nasdaq that the company has regained compliance.
  • The company’s common stock will continue to be listed and traded on The Nasdaq Capital Market.

data.title: NetClass Technology buys 51% stake in Japan's CreateSolutions, shares up
data.description:
• NetClass Technology (NASDAQ:NTCL) Monday announced its acquisition of a 51% stake in CreateSolutions Co, a Tokyo-based software company, by investing JPY 2,550,000 to acquire 255 shares.
  • The acquisition marks a significant step in the company’s strategy to expand its market across Asia, and provides NetClass Technology (NASDAQ:NTCL) with a new operational centre in Tokyo, Japan.

  • Shares up +7.01% premarket to $10.99.

data.title: OptimizeRx appoints Stephen Silvestro as CEO
data.description:
• OptimizeRx (NASDAQ:OPRX) said on Monday it has appointed Steve Silvestro as the company’s new chief executive officer, effective March 10, 2025.
  • Silvestro joined the company as chief commercial officer in April 2019, and has since served as president and, most recently, as interim CEO. Prior to joining the company, Silvestro held leadership positions at various companies including CCH® Tagetik, Prognos Health, Decision Resources Group. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eastside Distilling receives Nasdaq approval as a new listing
data.description:
• Eastside Distilling (NASDAQ:BLNE) said its application for a new Nasdaq listing has been officially accepted. 
  • The firm added the shareholders approved its name change to Beeline on March 7, with the change set to take effect on March 12. 
  • The company also said to meet the minimum price requirement for its new Nasdaq listing, Beeline will implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its common stock.

data.title: NET Power GAAP EPS of -$0.67, revenue of $0.25M
data.description:
• NET Power press release (NYSE:NPWR): FY GAAP EPS of -$0.67.
  • Revenue of $0.25M (+38.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Sunnova names Mathews new President and CEO, succeeding Berger
data.description:
Sunnova Energy (NYSE:NOVA) said Monday that current COO Paul Mathews has been appointed as its new President and CEO, effective immediately, succeeding John Berger, who is stepping down as Chairman, President and CEO.

Mathews joined Sunnova (NYSE:NOVA) in January 2023 and served as Executive VP and COO, after previously holding multiple leadership positions at United Parcel Service during 2004-22, including as President of Engineering during 2020-22.

Berger founded Sunnova (NOVA) 12 years ago and served as Chairman, President and CEO, building the company into one of the largest residential solar and storage providers in the U.S.

Sunnova (NOVA) shares have dropped 85% YTD and fell to an all-time low last week after reporting a larger than expected loss and issuing a going concern warning.

data.title: Bond traders brace for economic slowdown, Lutnick dismisses recession fears
data.description:
Bond traders have been preparing for a potential downturn in the U.S. economy in light of recent chaotic tariff decisions, even as the Trump administration seeks to deflect these concerns.

On Sunday, when asked if he was expecting a recession, President Donald Trump said he "hates to predict things like that" and said "there is a period of transition because what we're doing is very big."

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick affirmed that "there's going to be no recession in America." He told NBC News Trump plans to grow the U.S. economy "in a way we've never grown before."

If Trump's bringing growth to America, "I would never bet on recession, no chance," Lutnick said. He also insisted that while some foreign-made products might be more expensive, American products will get cheaper.

But bond traders appear to be more pessimistic and are increasingly buying short-dated Treasuries, with the two-year yield (US2Y) declining sharply over the last two weeks. The US2Y, which is most sensitive to interest rate outlook, is currently 5 basis points lower at 3.95%, while the 10-year yield (US10Y) is down 5 bps at 4.25%.

Kathy Jones, chief fixed income strategist at Schwab Center for Financial Research, last week noted that trade barriers and immigration limits can potentially raise inflation in the short run and slow growth in the long run. "It looks like the bond market has decided to bypass short-term inflation concerns and focus on the long-term prospects."

The odds of a recession remain low, but there are signs of economic slowdown. The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model expects the economy to contract 2.4% in the first quarter.

"While the headline number is scary, and those more pessimistic have used it to build their case for an imminent recession, the details are far less ominous," Investing Group Leader Lawrence Fuller said. "The sharp contraction was due largely to a huge trade deficit at the start of the year, which subtracted approximately 3.5% from GDP, as U.S. businesses ramped imports to front run Trump's tariffs."

data.title: M&A Snapshot: Rocket Companies acquires Redfin in $1.75B all-stock deal
Rocket agrees to buy Redfin for $1.75B
data.description:
Rocket Cos. (NYSE:RKT), parent of Rocket Mortgage, agreed to acquire real estate broker Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) in a deal valued at $1.75B that will combine one of the most-visited brokerage websites with the largest mortgage lender, the companies said Monday.

The online lending platform will pay $12.50 per RDFN share in an all stock transaction, representing a premium of ~63% over the volume-weighted average of the stock for 30 days through Friday. Under the terms, each share of Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) common stock will be exchanged for a fixed ratio of 0.7926 shares of Rocket Cos. (NYSE:RKT) class A common stock.

Redfin (RDFN) stock surged 81% to $10.52 in premarket trading, while Rocket (RKT) shares slid 8.4%.

Redfin's (RDFN) home search platform lists more than 1M for-sale and rental listings, as well as a tech-powered brokerage network of more than 2,200 agents. The purchase would add to Rocket's (RKT) pipeline of potential borrowers for its mortgages business.

"Together, we will improve the experience by connecting traditionally disparate steps of the search and financing process with leading technology that removes friction, reduces costs and increases value to American homebuyers," said Rocket CEO Varun Krishna.

The transaction is expected to add to Rocket's (RKT) adjusted EPS by the end of 2026 and to produce more than $200M in run-rate synergies by 2027.

As part of the deal, Rocket (RKT) will collapse its current Up-C structure to simplify its capital structure. Following the collapse, the public stockholders will continue to hold their current shares of common stock, while Dan Gilbert and the other stockholders of Rock Holdings Inc. ("RHI") will hold shares of common stock directly in Rocket Companies, instead of through RHI, and will no longer hold limited liability company units of Rocket, LLC.

In connection with the Up-C collapse, the Rocket (RKT) board declared a cash dividend of $0.80 per share to holders of Rocket's class A common stock to be paid on April 3, 2025, to holders of record at the close of March 20, 2025.

The deal is expected to close in Q2 or Q3 of 2025.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.
